title: Isaiah.

source.title: NPNF2-06. Jerome: The Principal Works of St. Jerome

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: Addressed to Paula and Eustochium, about a.d.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: This Preface speaks of Isaiah as using the polished diction natural to a man of rank and refinement, as an Evangelist more than a prophet, and a poet rather than a prose writer.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: He then reiterates his defence of his translation, saying that now, “The Jews can no longer scoff at our Churches because of the falsity of our Scriptures.”
